title: Component embedding in thinner core using dielectric sheet
abstract: A component carrier (400), comprising a core (100) having a recess (102), at least one electronic component (306) arranged in the recess (102), wherein a vertical thickness (D) of the at least one electronic component (306) is larger than a vertical thickness (d) of the core (100), and an electrically insulating sheet (402) covering at least part of a top main surface (110) of the core (100), covering at least part of the at least one electronic component (306) and filling a gap between a lateral surface (300) of the at least one electronic component (306) and a lateral surface (104) of the core (100) in the recess (102).
claims:
Claims C l a i m s 1. A component carrier (400), comprising : a core (100) having a recess (102); at least one electronic component (306) arranged in the recess (102), wherein a vertical thickness (D) of the at least one electronic component (306) is larger than a vertical thickness (d) of the core (100); an electrically insulating sheet (402) covering at least part of a top main surface (110) of the core (100), covering at least part of the at least one electronic component (306) and filling a gap between a lateral surface (300) of the at least one electronic component (306) and a lateral surface (104) of the core (100) in the recess (102). 2. The component carrier (400) according to claim 1, wherein the electrically insulating sheet (402) is laminated with the core (100) and the at least one electronic component (306). 3. The component carrier (400) according to claim 1 or 2, comprising a further electrically insulating layer structure (600) formed, in particular laminated, on top of the sheet (402). 4. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein a bottom main surface (304) of the at least one electronic component (306) is flush with a bottom main surface (106) of the core (100). 5. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the sheet (402) covers the upper surface (302) of the at least one electronic component (306). 6. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the at least one electronic component (306) comprises at least two vertically stacked electronic components (306) having a total vertical thickness (D) being larger than a vertical thickness (d) of the core (100). 7. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the at least one electronic component (306) is entirely surrounded by the same material, in particular by resin material. 8. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 7, comprising another electrically insulating layer structure (602), in particular a further core of fully cured material, laminated on bottom (106) of the core (100) and on bottom (304) of the at least one electronic component (306). 9. The component carrier (400) according to claim 8, wherein the further electrically insulating layer structure (600) and the other electrically insulating layer structure (602) are made of the same material. 10. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 8 or 9, wherein the sheet (402), the further electrically insulating layer structure (600) and the other electrically insulating layer structure (602) have substantially the same coefficient of thermal expansion. 11. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 8 to 10, wherein the sheet (402), the further electrically insulating layer structure (600) and the other electrically insulating layer structure (602) have a coefficient of thermal expansion of less than 20 ppm/Â°C, in particular less than 15 ppm/Â°C. 12. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the electrically insulating sheet (402) is an adhesive sheet (402), in particular comprising or consisting of one of the group consisting of resin, prepreg and Resin Coated Copper. 13. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 3 to 12, wherein the further electrically insulating layer structure (600) comprises at least part of at least one of prepreg and Resin Coated Copper. 14. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 13, wherein the core (100) comprises at least one electrically conductive vertical through connection (108), in particular made of copper. 15. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 14, wherein the core (100) comprises a fully cured dielectric, in particular FR4. 16. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 15, wherein a build-up on an upper main surface (404) of the sheet (402) is substantially symmetrical to another build-up on a lower main surface (106) of the core (100), the at least one electronic component (306) and the sheet (402). 17. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 16, wherein the recess (102) is free of additional adhesive material. 18. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 16, comprising additional adhesive material (800) in the recess (102), in particular between at least part of the at least one electronic component (306) and the core (100). 19. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 18, further comprising at least one electrically conductive layer structure (604) laminated on and/or within the component carrier (400). 20. The component carrier (400) according to claim 19, wherein the at least one electrically conductive layer structure (604) comprises at least one of the group consisting of copper, aluminum, and nickel. 21. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 20, wherein at least one of the sheet (402) and the further electrically insulating layer structure (600) comprises at least one of the group consisting of resin, in particular Bismaleimide-Triazine resin, cyanate ester, glass, in particular glass fibers, prepreg material, polyimide, polyamide, polytetrafluoroethylene, liquid 

 crystal polymer, epoxy-based Build-Up Film, FR4 material, FR5 material, a ceramic, and a metal oxide. 22. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 21, wherein the component carrier (400) is shaped as a plate. 23. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 22, wherein the component carrier (400) is configured as one of the group consisting of a printed circuit board, and a substrate. 24. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 23, wherein the electronic component (306) is selected from a group consisting of an active electronic component, a passive electronic component, an electronic chip, a storage device, a filter, an integrated circuit, a signal processing component, a power management component, an optoelectronic interface element, a voltage converter, a cryptographic component, a transmitter and/or receiver, an electromechanical transducer, a sensor, an actuator, a microelectromechanical system, a microprocessor, a capacitor, a resistor, an inductance, a battery, a switch, a camera, an antenna, a magnetic element and a logic chip. 25. The component carrier (400) according to any of claims 1 to 24, configured as a laminate-type component carrier (400). 26. A method of manufacturing a component carrier (400), the method comprising : providing a core (100) having a recess (102); arranging at least one an electronic component (306) in the recess (102), wherein a vertical thickness (D) of the at least one electronic compo- nent (306) is larger than a vertical thickness (d) of the core (100); connecting an electrically insulating sheet (402) to the core (100) and the at least one electronic component (306) so that material of the sheet (402) covers at least part of the core (100) and of the at least one electronic 

 component (306) and fills a gap between a lateral surface (300) of the at least one electronic component (306) and a lateral surface (104) of the core (100) in the recess (102). 5 27. The method according to claim 26, wherein the method further comprises connecting, in particular laminating, a further electrically insulating layer structure (600) on top of the sheet (402). 28. The method according to claim 26 or 27, wherein the at least one electronic) ic component (306) is arranged in the recess (102) so that a bottom main surface (304) of the at least one electronic component (306) is aligned with a bottom main surface (106) of the core (100). 29. The method according to any of claims 26 to 28, wherein the sheet (402) 15 is provided as a continuous layer to cover also an upper surface (302) of the at least one electronic component (306). 30. The method according to claim 29, wherein the sheet (402) has a blind hole type indentation (410) at the position of and at a main surface facing the 0 at least one electronic component (306). 31. The method according to any of claims 26 to 28, wherein the sheet (402) is provided as a recessed layer having a recess (900), in particular a through hole, to accommodate an upper portion of the at least one electronic compo- 5 nent (306). 32. The method according to any of claims 26 to 31, wherein the method further comprises laminating an adhesive tape (200) to the core (100) before arranging the at least one electronic component (306) in the core (100) and 30 adhering the at least one electronic component (306) to the adhesive tape (200). 33. The method according to claim 32, wherein the method further comprises removing the adhesive tape (200) after laminating the sheet (402). 34. The method according to any of claims 27 to 33, wherein the further electrically insulating layer structure (600) is a further core. 35. The method according to any of claims 26 to 34, wherein the electrically insulating sheet (402) is laminated with the core (100) and the at least one electronic component (306). 36. The method according to claim 35, wherein before laminating the electrically insulating sheet (402), the electrically insulating sheet (402) comprises at least partially uncured material which is cured during laminating the electrically insulating sheet (402).
